Name: curvature_aware_adaptive_precision_routing
Title: Curvature-Aware Adaptive Precision Routing for Efficient Machine Learning
Short Hypothesis: By integrating curvature diagnostics into an adaptive precision routing framework, we can optimize computational overhead while maintaining model stability and performance, offering a systematic approach distinct from existing methods.
Related Work: Existing works primarily focus on curvature-aware optimization strategies for specific tasks, such as derivative-free optimization and continual learning. This proposal distinguishes itself by proposing a comprehensive framework that combines adaptive precision routing with curvature diagnostics across various machine learning models, rather than targeting isolated optimization problems.
Abstract: In this proposal, we introduce a novel Curvature-Aware Adaptive Precision Routing Framework that dynamically adjusts computational precision based on curvature diagnostics of the loss landscape. Our approach aims to enhance the efficiency and stability of machine learning models during training and inference, particularly in single-GPU settings. By leveraging curvature information, we can inform precision tiering decisions that optimize computational overhead while preserving model performance. We will empirically validate our framework using benchmark datasets and architectures, demonstrating its effectiveness in various scenarios. Our findings are expected to advance the understanding of the relationship between precision, stability, and performance in machine learning, contributing to more efficient model deployment strategies.
Experiments: 1. Implement the proposed framework within standard neural network architectures (e.g., CNNs, MLPs) and benchmark against conventional fixed-precision training.
2. Quantify the effects of adaptive precision routing on training stability and convergence rates using metrics such as loss curves and gradient covariance decay.
3. Evaluate model performance on standard datasets (e.g., CIFAR-10, MNIST) while measuring computational overhead and resource utilization (e.g., GPU memory usage, training time).
4. Conduct ablation studies to isolate the impact of curvature diagnostics on precision routing decisions and overall model performance.
Risk Factors and Limitations: 1. Potential difficulty in accurately estimating curvature information, which may affect precision routing decisions.
2. The effectiveness of the framework may vary across different models or datasets, requiring careful tuning of hyperparameters.
3. Possible trade-offs between precision and computational efficiency that need to be thoroughly analyzed and understood.
